message:
feat(lab): wire LabReactor into LabRuntime for deterministic I/O simulation

Integrate the lab reactor into the lab runtime so that deterministic I/O
tests can use a fully wired reactor without manual setup:

- Add `lab_reactor` field (Arc<LabReactor>) to LabRuntime struct
- Construct LabReactor in LabRuntime::new(), using ChaosConfig when
  available (LabReactor::with_chaos) or plain LabReactor::new() otherwise
- Pass the reactor to RuntimeState::with_reactor() so the runtime's
  IoDriver is backed by the lab reactor from initialization
- Add lab_reactor() accessor with #[must_use] for test injection of
  deterministic I/O events
- Advance the lab reactor's virtual time in advance_time() and
  advance_time_to() so scheduled I/O completions fire at the correct
  virtual timestamps
- Add poll_io() helper that calls io_driver.turn(Duration::ZERO) each
  step to drain any pending I/O completions before scheduling tasks
- Call poll_io() at the start of each step() iteration so I/O
  completions from the previous time advance are visible to tasks
  before they run

This completes the runtime integration side of the Reactor trait
(asupersync-ds8.2.1), enabling the lab runtime to drive deterministic
I/O workloads through the same IoDriver/Reactor abstraction used by
production backends.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
